summary: M5-MANUAL-NODE-BATTLE-DECISION-001 is CLOSED after PR #125 passed required CI, merged into origin/main at daf09128b3c15bee26664a9b750a625ed8384767, and local main synchronized to origin/main. The accepted product-owner decision is DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE. Manual node battle remains outside M5 and the 1.0 mainline; no implementation task or blocking spike/research task is authorized for M5/M6/M7/M8.

tests_run.result: Quality, Web, and Browser Smoke passed in 1m49s; Windows Desktop Smoke Artifact passed in 53s.
Merged PR #125 and fast-forwarded local main to daf09128b3c15bee26664a9b750a625ed8384767.

tests_run.command: git rev-parse HEAD; git rev-parse main; git rev-parse origin/main
tests_run.result: All resolved to daf09128b3c15bee26664a9b750a625ed8384767 before closure branch edits.
M5-EXIT-HUMAN-GATE-001 was the next dependency-ready task after PR #125 entered main.

decisions: Close M5-MANUAL-NODE-BATTLE-DECISION-001 because the accepted decision and required reviewer evidence are integrated into origin/main through PR #125.
Keep manual node battle deferred from M5 and the 1.0 mainline.
Start M5-EXIT-HUMAN-GATE-001 as the next separate formal M5 passage gate; this closure does not pass M5 and does not start M6.
risks: M5 Exit remains a Human Gate and must produce its own evidence and acceptance before M5 can pass.
Manual node battle may appear only as a post-1.0 candidate or future research candidate; that status remains non-authorizing for the 1.0 task graph.
requested_action: Integrate this closure/start checkpoint, then continue M5-EXIT-HUMAN-GATE-001 gate evaluation without treating M5 as passed.
